Australia

Australian Government Fully Funded Awards for Migrant and Refugee Students

Published 16 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 16 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Australian Government Fully Funded Awards

The Australian Government offers fully funded awards to migrant and refugee students to support their education in Australia, providing them with an opportunity to pursue their academic goals without financial burdens. These awards are available for both undergraduate and postgraduate studies, covering various fields of study. By applying for these awards, migrant and refugee students can achieve their academic aspirations and build a better future for themselves.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for the Australian Government fully funded awards, migrant and refugee students must hold one of the following visa categories:

* Permanent Humanitarian Visa (subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204, 206, 207, 208, 209, 210, 211, 212, 213, 214, 215, 216, 217, 818, or 820)

* Temporary Protection Visa (subclass 785)

* Safe Haven Enterprise Visa (subclass 790)

* Temporary Humanitarian Concern Visa (subclass 786)

* Protection Visa (subclass 866)

Available Scholarships

The Australian Government offers various fully funded awards to support the education of migrant and refugee students. Here are five real scholarships available:

Australia Awards Scholarships

The Australia Awards Scholarships provide full tuition fees, living expenses, and other allowances up to AUD 30,000 per year. To be eligible, applicants must hold a valid visa, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and apply for a participating university. The deadline for applications is typically in April each year. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au.

Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarships

The Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarships offer full tuition fees and living expenses up to AUD 28,597 per year. Eligible applicants must apply for a higher degree by research at a participating university, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and hold a valid visa. The deadline for applications varies depending on the university. For more information, visit https://www.education.gov.au/research-training-program.

Australian Government Research Training Program (AGRTP) International Scholarships

The Australian Government Research Training Program (AGRTP) International Scholarships provide full tuition fees and living expenses up to AUD 28,597 per year. To be eligible, applicants must apply for a higher degree by research at a participating university, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and hold a valid visa. The deadline for applications is typically in August each year. For more information, visit https://www.unisa.edu.au/Research/Research-degrees/Scholarships/.

Endeavour Scholarships and Fellowships

The Endeavour Scholarships and Fellowships offer full tuition fees, living expenses, and travel allowances up to AUD 140,000. Eligible applicants must apply for a postgraduate program or a research fellowship at a participating university, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and hold a valid visa. The deadline for applications is typically in June each year. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au/endavour.

International Postgraduate Research Scholarship (IPRS)

The International Postgraduate Research Scholarship (IPRS) provides full tuition fees and living expenses up to AUD 28,597 per year. To be eligible, applicants must apply for a higher degree by research at a participating university, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and hold a valid visa. The deadline for applications varies depending on the university. For more information, visit https://www.education.gov.au/international-postgraduate-research-scholarship.

Application Steps

To apply for the Australian Government fully funded awards, migrant and refugee students can follow these steps:

  • Check the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and ensure they meet the requirements.

  • Choose a participating university and program of study.

  • Prepare the necessary documents, including academic transcripts, language proficiency test results, and visa documents.

  • Submit an application for the scholarship through the university's website or the official government website.

  • Apply for a valid visa to study in Australia.

  • Meet the English language proficiency requirements.

  • Provide proof of financial support, if required.

  • Submit the application before the deadline.

Documents Checklist

Migrant and refugee students should prepare the following documents to apply for the Australian Government fully funded awards:

* Academic transcripts

* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS, TOEFL)

* Visa documents (e.g. passport, visa approval letter)

* Proof of financial support (e.g. bank statement, scholarship award letter)

* Personal statement or research proposal

* Letters of recommendation

* Medical certificate

* Police certificate

Frequently Asked Questions

Here are some frequently asked questions about the Australian Government fully funded awards:

  • What is the eligibility criteria for the Australian Government fully funded awards?

The eligibility criteria vary depending on the scholarship, but generally, applicants must hold a valid visa, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and apply for a participating university.

  • How do I apply for the Australian Government fully funded awards?

Applicants can submit an application through the university's website or the official government website, and must provide the necessary documents, including academic transcripts, language proficiency test results, and visa documents.

  • What is the deadline for applying for the Australian Government fully funded awards?

The deadline varies depending on the scholarship and university, but generally, applications are accepted between April and August each year.

  • Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?

Yes, applicants can apply for multiple scholarships, but must meet the eligibility criteria for each scholarship and submit separate applications.

  • How will I be notified if I am selected for a scholarship?

Applicants will be notified by email or mail if they are selected for a scholarship, and will be required to accept the award and provide additional documentation.

  • Can I defer my scholarship if I am unable to start my studies on time?

Yes, applicants may be able to defer their scholarship, but must provide evidence of exceptional circumstances and receive approval from the university and the government.

  • How long does it take to process a scholarship application?

The processing time varies depending on the scholarship and university, but generally, applications are processed within 2-6 months.

  • What are the conditions of the scholarship?

The conditions of the scholarship vary depending on the award, but generally, recipients must maintain good academic standing, comply with the terms of their visa, and provide progress reports to the university and the government.

External Resources

For more information about the Australian Government fully funded awards, visit the Australian Government Department of Education, Skills and Employment website or the University of South Australia website.

You can find more information about scholarships and opportunities for migrant and refugee students on our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
